Predicate argument has wrong type:
  atLocation credit_card coffee_table: credit_card is not a agent
attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Examine a credit card by the light of a tall lamp.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene contains a wooden coffee table with a smooth, light-brown surface, positioned on top of a large, ornate area rug with a dominant orange background and intricate floral patterns in blue, beige, and dark brown. The rug covers a herringbone-patterned wooden floor. On the table’s surface, there is a small, rectangular red object identifiable as a credit card, located near the top-left corner in the first image and shifted toward the center-right in the second image. Two dark, circular metallic rings are also present on the table — one near the top-left and another near the top-right — but these are not relevant to the domain types.

In the third image, a tall lamp is shown in close-up. It features a textured, olive-green conical lampshade with a grid-like pattern and a visible dark rim at its base. The lamp’s stand appears to be made of a light-colored material, possibly wood or painted metal, and is partially visible beneath the shade. The lamp is positioned against a plain, light-brown wall.

The credit card is an “object” as defined in the domain. The coffee table is not explicitly defined as a “receptacle” in the domain, but for the purpose of this task, it can be treated as a surface upon which objects rest — though no domain predicate directly models “on” for non-receptacle surfaces. The lamp is also an “object” and can be toggled (turned on/off) as per the domain’s ToggleObject action.

The robot is initially not at any object’s location. The credit card is currently not held by the agent and is resting on the table. The lamp is currently off (not “isOn”), and its state can be changed via the ToggleObject action. The goal is to examine the credit card by the light of the tall lamp, which implies the robot must first go to the lamp, toggle it on, then go to the credit card, pick it up, and bring it near the lamp (or vice versa) to fulfill the “examine by light” condition. The lamp is not a receptacle and cannot hold objects; it is an independent object with a toggle state.

No microwave, fridge, sink, or knife is visible in the scene. The only relevant objects for the task are the credit card (object), the tall lamp (object), and the coffee table (surface, not a receptacle per domain). The spatial relationship is that the credit card is on the table, and the lamp is nearby (as implied by the context of the images and the instruction). The robot must navigate between these objects to complete the task.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem examine_credit_card_by_lamp)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        credit_card - object
        tall_lamp - object
        coffee_table - object
    )
    (:init
        (not (atLocation robot credit_card))
        (not (atLocation robot tall_lamp))
        (not (atLocation robot coffee_table))
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (isOn tall_lamp))
        (not (isToggled tall_lamp))
        (not (holds robot credit_card))
        (atLocation credit_card coffee_table)
    )
    (:goal (and
        (isOn tall_lamp)
        (holds robot credit_card)
        (atLocation robot tall_lamp)
    ))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem examine_credit_card_by_lamp)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        credit_card - object
        tall_lamp - object
        coffee_table - object
    )
    (:init
        (not (atLocation robot credit_card))
        (not (atLocation robot tall_lamp))
        (not (atLocation robot coffee_table))
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (isOn tall_lamp))
        (not (isToggled tall_lamp))
        (not (holds robot credit_card))
        (atLocation credit_card coffee_table)
    )
    (:goal (and
        (isOn tall_lamp)
        (holds robot credit_card)
        (atLocation robot tall_lamp)
    ))
)

The robot is not currently at any object’s location. The credit card is on the table, and the lamp is nearby but not on the table. The robot must first navigate to the table to pick up the credit card, then move to the lamp, and toggle the lamp on to examine the card by its light.

- Credit card: on table, not held, not cleaned, not heated, not cooled, not sliced.
- Lamp: not toggled on, not held, not in any receptacle.
- Table: surface object, not a receptacle per domain.
- No agent is present or holding any object.
- No receptacles are visible or interacted with.

The instruction requires the robot to pick up the credit card, go to the lamp, toggle the lamp on, and then examine the card under its light—implying the card should be held near the lamp while the lamp is on.

attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem examine_credit_card)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        credit_card - object
        lamp - object
        coffee_table - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot coffee_table)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (isOn lamp))
        (not (isToggled lamp))
        (not (holds robot credit_card))
        (not (inReceptacle credit_card coffee_table))
        (not (isClean credit_card))
        (not (isHot credit_card))
        (not (isCool credit_card))
        (not (isSliced credit_card))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (holds robot credit_card)
        (atLocation robot lamp)
        (isOn lamp)
    ))
)
```

        (not (isSliced credit_card))

    (:goal (and

)

```pddl
(define (problem examine_credit_card)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        credit_card - object
        lamp - object
        coffee_table - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot coffee_table)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (isOn lamp))
        (not (isToggled lamp))
        (not (holds robot credit_card))
        (not (inReceptacle credit_card coffee_table))
        (not (isClean credit_card))
        (not (isHot credit_card))
        (not (isCool credit_card))
        (not (isSliced credit_card))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (holds robot credit_card)
        (atLocation robot lamp)
        (isOn lamp)
    ))
)
```
final.problem:
(define (problem examine_credit_card)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        credit_card - object
        lamp - object
        coffee_table - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot coffee_table)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (isOn lamp))
        (not (isToggled lamp))
        (not (holds robot credit_card))
        (not (inReceptacle credit_card coffee_table))
        (not (isClean credit_card))
        (not (isHot credit_card))
        (not (isCool credit_card))
        (not (isSliced credit_card))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (holds robot credit_card)
        (atLocation robot lamp)
        (isOn lamp)
    ))
)
